thread: Will the Convocation and wider institutions recognize the threat in time?

evidence.clue: Sylvanas spends the opening trying to avoid a Convocation session about patrol cuts.
Jaina already brought her readings to senior authorities and was asked for more proof instead of action.
Millhaven finally generates hard enough evidence that Sylvanas sends a formal warning north under Neral's hand.
At Strahnbrad, Arthas hears the full warning, reads the notes, identifies Andorhal as the center, and still refuses to divert riders from the visible war.
Arthas offers only to mention the threat in his next report, proving that comprehension and timely action are no longer the same thing.
Corin has already sent multiple notices through local committees, the district prefect, and Dalaran with no useful response.
Havel's district reports were written and ignored long enough for the hidden cell to entrench itself inside Andorhal's grain district.

readerKnows: The institutional problem now spans multiple layers: elven politics, human command refusal, local committees, and Dalaran's paperwork all fail in their own ways.
povCharacterKnows: Jaina and Sylvanas know they are operating inside a city that has already been warning itself unsuccessfully. Proof will matter only if they can drag it out of the bureaucracy and into action by force.
notes: This is no longer only about delay. It is about systems built to absorb correct alarms without changing course.
